**Igor Pokrajac** (born 2 January 1979) is a Croatian [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts"), who formerly competed in the [Light Heavyweight](/wiki/Light_Heavyweight_(MMA) "Light Heavyweight (MMA)") division of the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"). A professional competitor since 2003, Pokrajac has also formerly competed for [K-1](/wiki/K-1 "K-1"), [KSW](/wiki/Konfrontacja_Sztuk_Walki "Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki"), [It's Showtime](/wiki/It%27s_Showtime_(kickboxing) "It's Showtime (kickboxing)"), and [Jungle Fight](/wiki/Jungle_Fight "Jungle Fight") promotions.

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Igor_Pokrajac&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Igor_Pokrajac&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early career")]

Pokrajac started his professional MMA career in 2003, primarily competing in his native Croatia. Over the next six years, he compiled an impressive record of 21–5.

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Igor_Pokrajac&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

In August 2009, it was announced he had signed with the UFC, with his first fight taking place against [Vladimir Matyushenko](/wiki/Vladimir_Matyushenko "Vladimir Matyushenko") on September 19, 2009, at [UFC 103](/wiki/UFC_103 "UFC 103").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.

Pokrajac next faced [James Te-Huna](/wiki/James_Te-Huna "James Te-Huna") on February 21, 2010, at [UFC 110](/wiki/UFC_110 "UFC 110").[[3]](#cite_note-3) Pokrajac lost again, this time via third-round TKO, due to strikes. This loss was somewhat controversial, as Te Huna had Pokrajac in a modified crucifix position on the mat and landed five to six clean shots to Pokrajac's face and although Pokrajac was not able to defend himself from this position, he was signalling to the referee that he was okay, although the ref did not acknowledge this, and stopped the fight after the unanswered shots from Te Huna. Although visibly dazed, Pokrajac disputed the stoppage directly after the fight to no avail.

Pokrajac then faced [James Irvin](/wiki/James_Irvin_(fighter) "James Irvin (fighter)") on August 1, 2010, at [UFC on Versus 2](/wiki/UFC_on_Versus_2 "UFC on Versus 2").[[4]](#cite_note-4) Midway through the first round, Pokrajac took Irvin down and immediately got side control on the floor. After taking multiple strikes, Irvin rolled over and was submitted by a rear naked choke, handing Pokrajac his first UFC victory.

Pokrajac fought [Stephan Bonnar](/wiki/Stephan_Bonnar "Stephan Bonnar") on December 4, 2010, at [The Ultimate Fighter 12 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_GSP_vs._Team_Koscheck_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck Finale").[[5]](#cite_note-5) Pokrajac lost a unanimous decision.

Pokrajac defeated Todd Brown on March 3, 2011, at [UFC Live: Sanchez vs. Kampmann](/wiki/UFC_Live:_Sanchez_vs._Kampmann "UFC Live: Sanchez vs. Kampmann") by TKO at the conclusion of the first round. Brown was dropped by a knee in the final minute and hit with several clean follow up shots, being saved by the end of the round. When the fighters went back to their corners, Brown was unable to stand on his own power and the fight was stopped.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Pokrajac was expected to face [Krzysztof Soszynski](/wiki/Krzysztof_Soszynski "Krzysztof Soszynski") on June 11, 2011, at [UFC 131](/wiki/UFC_131 "UFC 131"), replacing an injured [Anthony Perosh](/wiki/Anthony_Perosh "Anthony Perosh").[[7]](#cite_note-7) However, Pokrajac was himself forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by returning UFC veteran [Mike Massenzio](/wiki/Mike_Massenzio "Mike Massenzio").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Pokrajac/Soszynski ultimately took place on December 10, 2011, at [UFC 140](/wiki/UFC_140 "UFC 140").[[9]](#cite_note-9) Pokrajac defeated Soszynski via first-round KO.

Pokrajac was expected to face [Thiago Silva](/wiki/Thiago_Silva_(fighter) "Thiago Silva (fighter)") on May 15, 2012, at [UFC on Fuel TV: Korean Zombie vs. Poirier](/wiki/2012_in_UFC#UFC_on_Fuel_TV:_The_Korean_Zombie_vs._Poirier "2012 in UFC"), replacing an injured [Brandon Vera](/wiki/Brandon_Vera "Brandon Vera").[[10]](#cite_note-10) However, Silva was pulled from the bout to face [Alexander Gustafsson](/wiki/Alexander_Gustafsson "Alexander Gustafsson") on April 14, 2012, at [UFC on Fuel TV 2](/wiki/UFC_on_Fuel_TV_2 "UFC on Fuel TV 2") after Gustafsson's original opponent [Antônio Rogério Nogueira](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Rog%C3%A9rio_Nogueira "Antônio Rogério Nogueira") pulled out of the bout citing an injury.[[11]](#cite_note-11) Pokrajac instead faced [Fábio Maldonado](/wiki/F%C3%A1bio_Maldonado "Fábio Maldonado") at the event.[[12]](#cite_note-Pokrajac/Maldonado-12) He won the fight via unanimous decision (29–28, 30–27, and 29–28).

Pokrajac was defeated by [Vinny Magalhães](/wiki/Vinny_Magalh%C3%A3es "Vinny Magalhães") via second round armbar on September 22, 2012, at [UFC 152](/wiki/UFC_152 "UFC 152").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Pokrajac next faced [Joey Beltran](/wiki/Joey_Beltran "Joey Beltran") on December 15, 2012, at [UFC on FX 6](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_The_Smashes#The_Ultimate_Fighter:_The_Smashes_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: The Smashes"), replacing an injured [Anthony Perosh](/wiki/Anthony_Perosh "Anthony Perosh").[[14]](#cite_note-PokrajacIn-14) Pokrajac lost the fight via unanimous decision. However, on January 10, 2013, it was announced that Beltran had failed his post fight drug test, testing positive for [nandrolone](/wiki/Nandrolone "Nandrolone") and the result of the bout was changed to No Contest.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

Pokrajac faced [Ryan Jimmo](/wiki/Ryan_Jimmo "Ryan Jimmo") at [UFC 161](/wiki/UFC_161 "UFC 161") in Winnipeg, Manitoba on June 15, 2013.[[16]](#cite_note-16) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.

Pokrajac faced [Rafael Cavalcante](/wiki/Rafael_Cavalcante "Rafael Cavalcante") on November 9, 2013, at [UFC Fight Night 32](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_32 "UFC Fight Night 32").[[17]](#cite_note-17) He lost via submission due to strikes in the first round.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

Pokrajac faced [Marcos Rogério de Lima](/wiki/Marcos_Rog%C3%A9rio_de_Lima "Marcos Rogério de Lima") on December 20, 2014, at [UFC Fight Night 58](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_58 "UFC Fight Night 58").[[19]](#cite_note-19) He lost the fight via TKO in the first round, and was subsequently released from the promotion shortly after.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

### Post UFC

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Igor_Pokrajac&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Post UFC")]

After being released from [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") Pokrajac signed for Croatia's [Final Fight Championship](/wiki/Final_Fight_Championship "Final Fight Championship") promotion. His debut was expected to be against Maciej Browarski, but he was replaced and he will now make his debut in heavyweight division against Archontis Taxiarchis at FFC 18 - Ljubljana on 17 April 2015.[[21]](#cite_note-21) He weighed 102 kg at official weight ins.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

### Second UFC stint

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Igor_Pokrajac&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Second UFC stint")]

After recording three first round stoppage victories on the regional circuit across the [Balkans](/wiki/Balkans "Balkans"), Pokrajac was re-signed for a second stint in the UFC. He faced [Jan Błachowicz](/wiki/Jan_B%C5%82achowicz "Jan Błachowicz") on April 10, 2016, at [UFC Fight Night 86](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_86 "UFC Fight Night 86").[[23]](#cite_note-23) Pokrajac lost the fight via unanimous decision.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

Pokrajac was expected to face [Ed Herman](/wiki/Ed_Herman_(fighter) "Ed Herman (fighter)") on March 4, 2017, at [UFC 209](/wiki/UFC_209 "UFC 209").[[25]](#cite_note-25) However, Pokrajac pulled out of the fight in early February citing an injury, and was replaced by [Gadzhimurad Antigulov](/wiki/Gadzhimurad_Antigulov "Gadzhimurad Antigulov").[[26]](#cite_note-IPout-26)

On May 4, 2019, it was reported that Pokrajac was released by the UFC for a second time.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

## Accomplishments and championships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Igor_Pokrajac&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Accomplishments and championships")]

* **Serbian Battle Championship**
  + SBC Light Heavyweight Championship (one time)
